summary.intent: Fix OS-aware local LLM feature checks so that --all-features doesn't break macOS builds by enabling mutually exclusive Metal and CUDA backends simultaneously, then merge main to resolve PR conflicts and address review comments.
summary.outcome: Branch successfully merged with main, conflicts resolved, justfile updated with OS-aware lint/test/release-preflight recipes, PR review comments fixed (flag order, missing --workspace, OS-aware release-preflight), and changes pushed.

repo: AGENTS.md is a symlink to CLAUDE.md on main — conflicts involving it should take main's symlink version
local-llm-metal (macOS) and local-llm-cuda (Linux) are mutually exclusive GPU backends; --all-features fails on macOS because cuda requires nvcc
moltis-providers and moltis-gateway are the crates that have local-llm-cuda/metal features and need special handling on macOS
Main's system_info.rs moved from crates/agents/ to crates/providers/ and switched from compile-time cfg! guards to runtime device detection, superseding the branch's approach
The project uses nightly toolchain + nextest (cargo nextest run) for tests
just lint and just test are the canonical OS-aware wrappers; contributors should use these instead of raw cargo commands

code.finding: lint and test recipes are OS-aware: on macOS, moltis-providers and moltis-gateway are excluded from --all-features runs and re-run separately with only local-llm-metal; release-preflight also needs the same OS-aware treatment
Main refactored CUDA detection to runtime rather than compile-time cfg! checks, making the branch's cfg!(not(target_os = "macos")) && cfg!(feature = "local-llm-cuda") approach unnecessary

workflow: When a PR shows merge conflicts, merging main into the feature branch (rather than rebasing) is an acceptable resolution strategy
PR review comments should be fetched via gh API and addressed before pushing final fixes
Flag order consistency matters in clippy invocations (--all-targets --all-features ordering should be uniform)

friction: PR showed 'tons of conflicts' requiring a full merge-from-main resolution session
AGENTS.md conflict was ambiguous until it was discovered to be a symlink on main
The branch's core Rust-level fix (cfg! CUDA guard) was made redundant by a parallel refactor on main, requiring the justfile approach to carry the full weight of the fix
Missing --workspace flag in Linux test branch was caught only in PR review, not locally
open_items: Verify CI passes on the pushed branch with the OS-aware justfile recipes
release-preflight OS-aware fix was added late (after PR review) — worth confirming it works correctly on both macOS and Linux
Runtime device detection in system_info.rs (main's approach) may need its own testing to ensure it correctly reports CUDA unavailable on macOS
